content:
Rork why can can complete my fucking chat room 100% end to end as senior developer what fuck is wrong with you fix now end to end audit now QA FINAL CHAT ROOM RECOVERY — FIX AI GATEWAY AUTHENTICATION END TO END NOW

The IVX Owner AI chat room is live, but assistant responses are failing with this exact production error:

IVXAIGatewayRequestError
providerError=Unauthenticated
endpoint=https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh/v3/ai/openai/gpt-4o
Configure AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY or use a provider module
origin=/app/backend/ivx-ai-runtime.ts:239:22

This is a backend AI provider authentication failure, not a frontend chat-layout issue.

EXECUTE NOW AS SENIOR DEVELOPER:

1. Audit the active production runtime that handles:
   /app/backend/ivx-ai-runtime.ts

2. Identify the exact deployed service and environment where the owner-room request executes.

3. Verify whether AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY exists in the active runtime environment.

4. Do not ask me to provide credentials unless the runtime proves the secret is:
   - missing
   - expired
   - revoked
   - deleted
   - returning 401 or 403

5. Audit all possible variable names and bindings:
   - AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY
   - OPENAI_API_KEY
   - VERCEL_AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY
   - AI_PROVIDER
   - AI_MODEL
   - AI_GATEWAY_URL

6. Confirm the code reads the same variable name that exists in the deployed environment.

7. Audit whether the secret is available:
   - at build time
   - at runtime
   - inside the correct Render service
   - inside the correct backend process
   - inside the owner-room execution path

8. Inspect /app/backend/ivx-ai-runtime.ts around line 239 and fix the provider initialization.

9. If Vercel AI Gateway is intentionally used:
   - bind AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY correctly
   - send the correct Authorization header
   - confirm the gateway URL and model format
   - verify the request with a real server-side test

10. If Vercel AI Gateway is not required:
   - switch the backend to the configured direct provider module
   - use the existing provider credential already stored in runtime variables
   - do not expose the key to the frontend

11. Add provider fallback logic:
   - primary configured provider
   - secondary configured provider
   - clear operational error only after both fail

12. Never return the raw internal stack trace to the user interface again.

13. Replace the current raw error card with:
   “IVX AI is temporarily unavailable. Retrying securely.”

14. Add server-side structured logging containing:
   - traceId
   - roomId
   - provider
   - model
   - HTTP status
   - sanitized provider error
   - timestamp
   Do not log any secret value.

15. Run an actual end-to-end owner-room test:
   - send a real message
   - verify assistant response
   - reload the app
   - verify conversation persists
   - send a second message
   - verify no duplicate, missing, delayed, or out-of-order messages

16. Verify the following chat functions:
   - send message
   - receive AI response
   - persistent history
   - realtime update
   - reload recovery
   - error recovery
   - typing/working state
   - scroll to newest message
   - no raw backend errors shown in UI

17. Commit and deploy the fix to production.

REQUIRED PROOF — NO NARRATIVE:

A. Root cause
B. Exact environment-variable name used by code
C. Exact environment where it was verified
D. Sanitized credential-binding result: PRESENT or MISSING
E. Provider selected
F. Model selected
G. Test request HTTP status
H. GitHub commit SHA
I. Render deployment ID
J. /health HTTP 200
K. Owner-room real message test
L. Assistant response proof
M. Conversation persistence proof after reload
N. Screenshot of working production chat
O. Final status:
   VERIFIED_COMPLETE
   or
   BLOCKED_WITH_EXACT_ERROR

Do not redesign the chat.
Do not create placeholders.
Do not ask for credentials before testing existing runtime variables.
Do not stop after changing code.
Fix, deploy, test, and prove the live owner chat works end to end now.
